No Poverty.reason: Course on pension plans directly supports social protection (Target 1.3). General insurance, especially catastrophe securitization, builds resilience against economic shocks and disasters (Target 1.5).
evidence: 公共及私人退休金計畫

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus relates to food security, agriculture, or nutrition.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course focuses on property/casualty insurance and pensions, not health insurance or direct health outcomes.

Quality Education.reason: The course provides advanced professional knowledge and skills in risk management and insurance, aligning with Target 4.4 for relevant skills for employment.

Gender Equality.reason: While 'business ethics' is mentioned, there is no specific evidence linking the course content to gender equality targets.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus relates to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus relates to energy access, renewables, or efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Pension plans support decent work (Target 8.5). The course strengthens financial insurance institutions, expanding access to financial services (Target 8.10).
evidence: 公共及私人退休金計畫

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The study of insurance and pension systems contributes to resilient financial infrastructure (Target 9.1) and provides financial services for enterprises (Target 9.3).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: Retirement fund management addresses economic inclusion for the elderly (Target 10.2) and supports social protection policies to reduce inequality (Target 10.4).

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus relates to marine ecosystems or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus relates to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.
